Which of the following is an example of conservation of energy?
multipleChoice: A person lifting a heavy box
questions.answer: A person lifting a heavy box

questions.question: Which of the following defines energy conservation?
multipleChoice: The transfer of energy from one form to another
The process of reducing energy consumption
The law that energy can never be destroyed
questions.answer: The law that energy can never be destroyed

questions.question: What is the equation for the conservation of energy?
Which of the following is NOT a form of energy?
How is energy conserved when a ball is thrown up into the air?
multipleChoice: Potential energy is converted to kinetic energy
Kinetic energy is converted to sound energy
Potential energy is converted to electrical energy
Kinetic energy is converted to potential energy
questions.answer: Kinetic energy is converted to potential energy

According to Newton’s First Law of Motion, which of the following must be true for an object to move?
multipleChoice: It must have a force acting upon it
It must be in a state of rest
It must be acted upon by a gravitational field
questions.answer: It must have a force acting upon it

questions.question: According to Newton’s Second Law of Motion, which of the following is true about an object’s acceleration?
multipleChoice: It is directly proportional to the force acting on it
It is inversely proportional to the mass of the object
It is inversely proportional to the force acting on it
It is directly proportional to the mass of the object
questions.answer: It is directly proportional to the force acting on it

questions.question: According to Newton’s Third Law of Motion, which of the following is true?
multipleChoice: For every action, there is an equal and opposite reaction
Objects at rest remain at rest
The force of gravity is proportional to the mass of the object
Objects in motion remain in motion
questions.answer: For every action, there is an equal and opposite reaction

questions.question: According to Newton’s First Law of Motion, if a force is applied to an object, then what will happen?
multipleChoice: The object will move in the same direction as the force
The object will remain at rest
The object will accelerate in the direction of the force
The object will move in the opposite direction of the force
questions.answer: The object will accelerate in the direction of the force

questions.question: According to Newton’s Second Law of Motion, how does the rate of acceleration of an object depend on its mass?
multipleChoice: It is directly proportional to the mass of the object
It is inversely proportional to the mass of the object
It is unaffected by the mass of the object
It is directly proportional to the force applied to the object
questions.answer: It is inversely proportional to the mass of the object

questions.question: According to Newton’s Third Law of Motion, what is the relationship between the forces acting on two objects?
multipleChoice: One force is equal and opposite to the other
The forces are equal and in the same direction
The forces are equal and in opposite directions
The forces are inversely proportional to each other
questions.answer: One force is equal and opposite to the other

questions.question: According to Newton’s First Law of Motion, what must be true for an object to remain in motion?
multipleChoice: It must be acted upon by a gravitational field
It must have a force acting upon it
It must be in a state of rest
questions.answer: It must have a force acting upon it

questions.question: According to Newton’s Second Law of Motion, how does the net force on an object affect its acceleration?
multipleChoice: It is directly proportional to the net force
It is inversely proportional to the net force
It is unaffected by the net force
It is directly proportional to the mass of the object
questions.answer: It is directly proportional to the net force

questions.question: According to Newton’s Third Law of Motion, what happens if two objects exert forces on each other?
multipleChoice: The forces cancel each other out
The forces are equal and opposite
The forces are inversely proportional to each other
The forces are equal and in the same direction
questions.answer: The forces are equal and opposite

questions.question: According to Newton’s First Law of Motion, what happens if a force is applied to an object at rest?
multipleChoice: The object will remain at rest
The object will move in the opposite direction of the force
The object will accelerate in the direction of the force
The object will move in the same direction as the force
questions.answer: The object will accelerate in the direction of the force

questions.question: According to Newton’s Second Law of Motion, what happens when a greater force is applied to an object?
multipleChoice: The object will experience a greater acceleration
The object will experience a smaller acceleration
The object will remain at rest
The object will experience no acceleration
questions.answer: The object will experience a greater acceleration

questions.question: According to Newton’s Third Law of Motion, what is the relationship between the forces on two objects?
multipleChoice: The forces are equal and in the same direction
The forces are inversely proportional to each other
The forces are equal and opposite
One force is equal and opposite to the other
questions.answer: One force is equal and opposite to the other

questions.question: According to Newton’s First Law of Motion, an object at rest will remain at rest unless _______?
multipleChoice: It is acted upon by a gravitational field
It is acted upon by a force
It is in a state of motion
questions.answer: It is acted upon by a force

questions.question: According to Newton’s Second Law of Motion, how does the acceleration of an object depend on its mass?
multipleChoice: It is directly proportional to the force applied to the object
It is inversely proportional to the mass of the object
It is unaffected by the mass of the object
It is directly proportional to the mass of the object
questions.answer: It is inversely proportional to the mass of the object

questions.question: According to Newton’s Third Law of Motion, what happens when two objects exert forces on each other?
multipleChoice: The forces are equal and in opposite directions
The forces are inversely proportional to each other

One force is equal and opposite to the other
questions.answer: One force is equal and opposite to the other
